label: Localized poison ivy dermatitis without infection or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized poison ivy dermatitis.
No anaphylaxis, infection, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye/genital location, burn or chemical wound, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or immunocompromised host.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, infection concern, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye or genital rash location, burn or chemical wound, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus contact dermatitis material supports irritant/allergic rash framing, trigger avoidance, washing exposed skin and objects, itch care, and escalation for infection, severe reaction, or concerning symptoms. This expansion is limited to localized poison ivy dermatitis without systemic allergic reaction, infection, eye/genital involvement, or severe cutaneous reaction.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized poison ivy dermatitis

high_confidence_terms: clinician diagnosis localized poison ivy dermatitis

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype.
